questions.question: This default can , -a , and -r options cause cp to copy recursively by descending into source directories and copying files to corresponding destination directories.

questions.question: Preserve as much , -a , and -r options cause cp to copy recursively by descending into source directories and copying files to corresponding destination directories.
questions.explanation: Preserve as much as possible of the structure and attributes of the original files in the copy (but do not attempt to preserve internal directory structure; i.e., ‘ ls -U ’ may list the entries in a copied directory in a different order).
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
